bugs_found: phi regalloc clash (3+ phis share register)

previous_rounds.summary: Task 3 tier-up policy flip caused fib/ackermann/mutual_recursion to hang at Tier 2; reverted. Task 2 (bounded recursive inlining, MaxRecursion=2) landed as dormant infrastructure.

Diagnosed tier2 hang: BuildGraph silently drops CALL B=0 args, producing exponentially wrong recursion depth. Landed Unpromotable gate so compileTier2 refuses such protos; policy flip reverted. 0 wall-time win, but latent crash now impossible + reproducer committed.

Phase 1 diagnostic: profiled all 5 float benchmarks (spectral_norm/nbody/matmul/mandelbrot/math_intensive). Top-3 hot patterns ranked: (1) per-op NaN box/unbox 5/5, (2) generic Mul/Add on any-typed loads 3/5, (3) redundant same-slot load 4/5. Escalation: shallow. Round-7 target: Pattern 1 (FPR-resident SSA across blocks).

Phase 2: FPR-resident infra (scratch-FPR operand cache + loop-header phi FPR carry into tight bodies). Mandelbrot -2.62% (0.382s->0.372s, far short of 35% target); aggregate -1.88% (41.98s->41.20s), zero regressions. Pivot to LICM (Phase 4) for loop-invariant materialisation.

Phase 4: LICM pass landed cleanly (hoists 17 consts from mandelbrot inc. ConstFloat 2/4 named in round 7). Validator clean, all tests pass, zero correctness regressions. But wall-time unchanged: mandelbrot -1.6% (0.387s->0.381s, target was -40%); LuaJIT-row aggregate ~+0.3%. Constant-rematerialisation was not the true B3 bottleneck; surviving FMUL/FADD chain dominates.
